description:
Machine-readable registry of all REST API endpoints and WebSocket events for the RFS Station Manager

All API endpoints are protected with rate limiting (≈1,000 requests per hour per IP by default (≈84 per 5-minute window), configurable via RATE_LIMIT_API_MAX environment variable).

Multi-Station Support: All endpoints support optional X-Station-Id header or stationId query parameter for station-based data filtering. Defaults to 'default-station' if not provided.

Authentication: When REQUIRE_AUTH=true, many endpoints require JWT authentication via Authorization: Bearer <token> header. See individual endpoint documentation for authentication requirements.

X-Station-Id.description: Optional station identifier for multi-station filtering. If not provided, defaults to 'default-station' for backward compatibility.

Authorization.description: JWT bearer token for authentication. Required for protected endpoints when REQUIRE_AUTH=true. Format: 'Bearer <token>'

billing.description: Stripe billing — Checkout, Customer Portal, status, webhook and audit trail. Owner-only except the signature-verified webhook. Returns 503 when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is not configured.

POST /api/billing/checkout.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session for the org's chosen plan and billing interval (14-day trial). Owner-only.

200.description: { checkoutUrl } — redirect the browser to complete payment

400.description: Invalid planCode/interval or no Stripe price configured for the combination

POST /api/billing/topup.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session (mode: payment) to purchase an AI session top-up pack. Credits aiBonusSessions on the org after webhook confirmation. Owner-only; only available when aiEnabled and STRIPE_PRICE_AI_TOPUP is configured.

200.description: { checkoutUrl, packSize } — redirect to complete payment

400.description: AI not enabled on this org

503.description: Top-up not configured (STRIPE_PRICE_AI_TOPUP or STRIPE_SECRET_KEY missing)

POST /api/billing/santa-addon/checkout.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session (mode: subscription) for the standalone Fire Santa Run add-on — for Community orgs whose plan doesn't already grant santaRunEnabled (Basic/AI Pro get it bundled). A$10/year (unlimited use) or A$15 for a one-off month. Owner-only; tracked on Organization.santaAddon independently of the org's main plan subscription.

400.description: Invalid billingInterval, price not configured for the chosen interval, or the org's plan already includes Fire Santa Run

503.description: Santa add-on not configured (STRIPE_PRICE_SANTA_ADDON_MONTHLY/_ANNUAL or STRIPE_SECRET_KEY missing)

POST /api/billing/portal.description: Create a Stripe Customer Portal session so the owner can manage their subscription/payment method. Owner-only.

400.description: Org has no Stripe customer yet (subscribe first)

GET /api/billing/status.description: Current billing summary for the authenticated user's org.

GET /api/billing/events.description: Owner-only audit trail of Stripe webhook events processed for this org (newest first).

POST /api/billing/webhook.description: Stripe webhook receiver. Verifies the Stripe-Signature against STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, syncs org plan/status/entitlements (checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.updated/deleted, invoice.paid, invoice.payment_failed), and writes an idempotent audit row. Requires the raw request body (express.raw registered before express.json).

200.description: { received: true } — always returned after audit; { duplicate: true } when the event was already processed

ai.description: Server-side AI gateway. Provider credentials live server-side; AAR Studio and the SPA call these instead of Azure browser-direct. When org context is present the aiEnabled entitlement is required; the monthly allowance (aiIncludedSessions) is metered in sessions and enforced at the speech-token endpoint. Anonymous calls (no org context) pass through deliberately — AAR Studio is local-first and usable signed-out (A2) — bounded instead by a dedicated per-IP aiRateLimiter (MASTER_PLAN Q31 / review F4), tighter than the general apiRateLimiter.
ai.authentication: JWT bearer (org context optional — no org context passes through, consistent with entitlement back-compat); all routes behind aiRateLimiter (30 req / 5 min per IP, tighter than the general apiRateLimiter)

POST /api/ai/chat.description: Proxy a chat/completion request to the configured AI provider (Azure OpenAI; Anthropic adapter stubbed). Requires aiEnabled when org context is present.

POST /api/ai/report.description: Generate an AI report/summary via the gateway. Requires aiEnabled when org context is present.

POST /api/ai/speech/token.description: Vend a short-lived Azure Speech authorization token for browser SDK use. Counts as one metered AI session and enforces the monthly allowance. A re-vend carrying a sessionId already recorded this month (a proactive token refresh or reconnect for the same review) does not consume a new session.

sessionId.description: Optional client-supplied review/meeting id. Vends sharing the same sessionId within the month are deduped to one metered session (AAR-11).

200.description: { token, region } — short-lived Speech auth token

402.description: Monthly AI session allowance (included + bonus) exhausted — { remaining, included, bonus, resetAt, topupAvailable }

GET /api/ai/usage.description: AI sessions used this month and the remaining monthly allowance for the org (drives the /admin/organization meter).

200.description: { used, included, bonus, remaining, resetAt, aiEnabled } — bonus is purchased top-up sessions not yet consumed

aar-sessions.description: Server-side persistence for AAR Studio reviews, scoped to the owning Organization so a review survives device loss and is visible brigade-wide instead of living only in one facilitator's localStorage. AAR Studio remains local-first: it works fully signed-out against localStorage and mirrors to these endpoints best-effort when an SM JWT is present. The session body is stored as an opaque JSON blob (payload) with a few fields lifted out for listing.

GET /api/aar-sessions.description: List this org's saved reviews as summaries (no payload), newest-updated first.

403.description: No organization context, or aarStudioEnabled entitlement required

GET /api/aar-sessions/:id.description: Fetch one saved review including the full payload, scoped to the caller's org.

403.description: No organization context, or aarStudioEnabled entitlement required

404.description: Review not found in this org

PUT /api/aar-sessions/:id.description: Create or replace a review (the client owns the id). Org scoping is enforced; creation metadata (createdAt/createdBy) is preserved across updates. Optimistic concurrency: when clientUpdatedAt is supplied and an existing row was edited more recently, the save is rejected (409) instead of clobbering it; omit clientUpdatedAt to force last-write-wins.

requestBody.title: string
requestBody.payload: string (JSON of the AAR Studio session)
requestBody.clientUpdatedAt: string? (the saving device's last-edit time, ISO; enables stale-write detection)

403.description: No organization context, or aarStudioEnabled entitlement required

409.description: Stale write — updated more recently on another device; body is { error, current } (current omits payload)

413.description: Review payload too large to persist (~900k char cap)

DELETE /api/aar-sessions/:id.description: Delete a review, scoped to the caller's org.

403.description: No organization context, or aarStudioEnabled entitlement required

404.description: Review not found in this org

truck-check-vehicle-types.description: TC-1 vehicle types + resolved checklists. A VehicleType owns a locked standard checklist (immutable for adopting brigades, stable itemCodes for cross-brigade comparison); isStandard publishes it for other orgs to adopt. An appliance links to a type via vehicleTypeId and the effective checklist = the type's standard items + the brigade's custom overlay, in saved order.
truck-check-vehicle-types.authentication: JWT (vehicle-type writes require admin + org ownership; the effective-checklist endpoints are kiosk-friendly)

GET /api/truck-checks/vehicle-types.description: List the org's vehicle types plus all published standards.

POST /api/truck-checks/vehicle-types.description: Create a vehicle type owned by the caller's org (isStandard publishes it).

PUT /api/truck-checks/vehicle-types/:id.description: Update a vehicle type the org owns (re-normalises standard item codes).

DELETE /api/truck-checks/vehicle-types/:.description: Delete a vehicle type the org owns.

GET /api/truck-checks/appliances/:id/che.description: The resolved effective checklist for an appliance (locked standard items + brigade custom overlay, in saved order). What the check workflow renders.

PUT /api/truck-checks/appliances/:id/che.description: Save the brigade's custom overlay (custom items + preferred order). Standard items are never sent here; they live on the type.

requestBody.itemOrder: string[] (standard item itemCode or custom item id)

truck-check-appliance-zones.description: A1 per-truck spatial model. An ApplianceZone is a named physical area on exactly ONE appliance (Pump Panel, Driver-side Locker 1, Cab), with a walk-around order and an optional canonical zoneCode slug for cross-brigade analytics. Admin-authored config; reads require an authenticated session. See docs/archive/AI_MAINTENANCE_AGENT_DESIGN.md §4.1.
truck-check-appliance-zones.authentication: JWT (zone writes require admin; list requires an authenticated session)

GET /api/truck-checks/appliances/:applia.description: List one appliance's zones in walk-around order.

POST /api/truck-checks/appliances/:appli.description: Create a zone on an appliance (stationId inherited from the truck; order defaults to the end; zoneCode derived from the name when not given).

PUT /api/truck-checks/zones/:id.description: Update a zone (partial patch; blank zoneCode is treated as not-provided).

POST /api/truck-checks/appliances/:id/se.description: Seed an appliance's zones from the starter vocabulary for its VehicleType.code (zoneVocabulary.ts). Idempotent by default — returns existing zones unchanged. Pass ?replace=true to delete and re-seed. Returns { seeded: number, zones: ApplianceZone[], message?: string }. Also triggered automatically (non-fatal) on POST /appliances when vehicleTypeId is supplied.

queryParams.replace: boolean? — if true, deletes existing zones before seeding

200.description: { seeded: number, zones: ApplianceZone[], message?: string }

422.description: No vehicleTypeId set on appliance, or vehicle type record not found

truck-check-appliance-equipment.description: A1 per-truck inventory. An ApplianceEquipment is a piece of equipment on exactly ONE appliance (Holmatro spreader, BA set #2, Akron branch), optionally located in a zone (zoneId). active:false retires an item without losing history. Admin-authored config; reads require an authenticated session. See docs/archive/AI_MAINTENANCE_AGENT_DESIGN.md §4.2.
truck-check-appliance-equipment.authentication: JWT (equipment writes require admin; list requires an authenticated session)

GET /api/truck-checks/appliances/:applia.description: List one appliance's equipment (active only by default; ?includeInactive=true to include retired).

POST /api/truck-checks/appliances/:appli.description: Add equipment to an appliance (stationId inherited from the truck; equipmentCode derived from the name when not given; active defaults true).

PUT /api/truck-checks/equipment/:id.description: Update an equipment item (partial patch; set active:false to retire while keeping history).

DELETE /api/truck-checks/equipment/:id.description: Delete an equipment item (prefer active:false to retain history).

truck-check-issues.description: TC-4 issue follow-up. A CheckResult with status 'issue' starts an 'open' lifecycle; an equipment officer moves it open → acknowledged → resolved (admin-gated via the parent mount; no separate role). Member attribution (TC-3): a check run's completedBy holds the roster memberId when the inspector is picked from the roster, else the free-text name (kiosk).

GET /api/truck-checks/issues.description: Station-scoped flat feed of issue results enriched with appliance/run context for the equipment-officer follow-up view. ?status=open|acknowledged|resolved; defaults to outstanding (open+acknowledged).

PATCH /api/truck-checks/results/:id/issu.description: Update an issue result's lifecycle (issueStatus/issueNote/assignedTo/resolvedBy). Resolving stamps resolvedAt. Body must include runId (Table Storage locates the result by run partition).

truck-check-runs.description: Check-run lifecycle. A run is created/joined via POST /api/truck-checks/runs, results are posted against it, and it is either completed (PUT /runs/:id/complete) or cancelled. Cancelling clears a run left open from a previous session so the vehicle is checkable again.

DELETE /api/truck-checks/runs/:id.description: Cancel/abandon a check run and cascade-delete its results. Used from the vehicle roster to clear a stuck in-progress run. Emits a station-scoped 'check-cancelled' truck-check-update socket event.

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/members.description: Get all registered members sorted by activity level

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/members/:id.description: Get specific member by ID. Requires a session (requireSession — admin JWT/brigade-token/member-session, or the public demo station). Station-scoped for brigade-token/member-session callers; an admin JWT instead matches on organizationId (Q9, 2026-07-17) so a deep link works regardless of the caller's currently-selected station.
GET /api/members/:id.authentication: requireSession (JWT any role / brigade-token / member-session / demo)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)
Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)
Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/members/:id/invite.description: Generate a one-time activation invite URL for a member (admin-only). Sets authStatus='invited' and inviteToken on the member. The returned inviteUrl points to /activate/:token. Token is consumed on first successful activation. Station-scoped, with the same organizationId-based cross-station allowance for admin JWTs as GET /api/members/:id (Q9, 2026-07-17).

email.description: Optional email to record on the member for reference

GET /api/members/activate/:token.description: Public endpoint. Validate an invite token and return member name and org name for the activation form. No authentication required.

404.description: Token not found or already used

POST /api/members/activate/:token.description: Public endpoint. Complete member activation: creates an AdminUser with role=viewer, marks Member.authStatus=active, clears inviteToken. No authentication required.

404.description: Token not found or already used

DELETE /api/members/:id.description: Soft delete a member (mark inactive, hide from UI, retain history). Station-scoped, with the same organizationId-based cross-station allowance for admin JWTs as GET /api/members/:id (Q9, 2026-07-17). Note: not itself behind requireSession (non-GET/HEAD, so only gated when ENABLE_DATA_PROTECTION=true) — kiosk write-path pass-through, matching the rest of the write surface.

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/members/:id/history.description: Get member's check-in history. Requires a session (requireSession); same organizationId-based cross-station allowance for admin JWTs as GET /api/members/:id (Q9, 2026-07-17).
GET /api/members/:id/history.authentication: requireSession (JWT any role / brigade-token / member-session / demo)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/members/import.description: Validate CSV file for bulk member import

file.description: CSV file with columns: First Name, Last Name, Rank, Roles

400.description: No file uploaded or CSV parsing failed

POST /api/members/import/execute.description: Execute bulk member import after validation

agent-sessions.description: Read access to A3 voice-agent sessions/transcripts (the write path is the /ws/agent-check WebSocket, documented separately under voiceAgentWebSocket). Mounted at /api/agent-sessions, rate limited, gated behind requireFeature('aiEnabled').

GET /api/agent-sessions/:id.authentication: JWT (organization-scoped: 404 if the session's organizationId does not match the caller's, A3 code review F2)

404.description: Not found, or not visible to the caller's organization

GET /api/agent-sessions/:id/turns.description: Fetch the ordered transcript turns for a session (used to rehydrate the PWA UI after a WS reconnect, A3 code review F9)
GET /api/agent-sessions/:id/turns.authentication: JWT (organization-scoped: 404 if the session's organizationId does not match the caller's, A3 code review F2)

404.description: Session not found, or not visible to the caller's organization

organizations.description: SaaS tenant management: plan/entitlements, direct user creation, invite links, and per-org membership (multi-org). All routes require authMiddleware; admin-only routes additionally require requireAdmin (owner is a superset).

GET /api/organizations/current.description: The caller's organization plus the plan catalog (for the upgrade UI).

PUT /api/organizations/current.description: Update name/billingEmail/planCode/moduleToggles/agencyName/agencyLogoUrl (owner only). Entitlements reset to the new plan's defaults on a real plan change, then owner module toggles are applied and clamped to the plan ceiling. D1 (2026-07-17): this is the only functional plan-upgrade path while Stripe billing is unconfigured, so a genuine plan change with no stripeSubscriptionId on the org also starts/ends a 14-day trial directly here — status: 'trialing' + trialEndsAt set on upgrade to a paid plan, status: 'active' + trialEndsAt cleared on downgrade to community. An org with a live Stripe subscription is untouched by this — its status/trialEndsAt stay governed exclusively by POST /api/billing/webhook. agencyName/agencyLogoUrl (2026-07-18): admin-configurable branding, defaulted from the claimed facility's serviceType at signup; an empty string clears the override.

moduleToggles.description: Partial<{signInEnabled, truckCheckEnabled, reportsEnabled, aiEnabled}> — narrows only, never exceeds the plan ceiling

agencyName.description: Up to 100 chars; empty string clears the override

agencyLogoUrl.description: Up to 500 chars; empty string clears the override

400.description: Invalid planCode, or agencyName/agencyLogoUrl not a string within the length limit

403.description: Caller is not the org owner

POST /api/organizations/current/invites.description: Create a shareable, multi-use invite link (default 7-day expiry, max 30) for the current org. Role limits enforced by orgMembershipRules.canInviteRole (owner mints any role; admin mints admin/viewer; viewer cannot invite).

email.description: Optional intended recipient — display only, no email is sent

403.description: Inviter's role cannot mint the requested invite role

GET /api/organizations/current/invites.description: List invite links for the current org (active/revoked/expired), with a live-computed inviteUrl per invite.

404.description: Invite not found in caller's org

GET /api/organizations/current/members.description: List members of the current org from OrganizationMembership rows joined with AdminUser, lazily materializing a membership row for any legacy user whose AdminUser.organizationId matches but has no row yet.

200.description: { members: [{userId, username, email, role, status, lastLoginAt, createdAt}] }

PUT /api/organizations/current/members/:.description: Change a member's role. Enforced by orgMembershipRules.canChangeRole and violatesLastOwner (an org must always retain ≥1 active owner). Also syncs AdminUser.role when this org is the target's default org.

403.description: Actor lacks permission for that role change, or it would remove the last owner

404.description: Member not found in this organisation

DELETE /api/organizations/current/member.description: Remove a member (or self — leave). Enforced by orgMembershipRules.canRemoveMember and violatesLastOwner. If the removed org was the user's default, repoints AdminUser.organizationId to their next active membership.

403.description: Actor lacks permission, or it would remove the last owner

404.description: Member not found in this organisation

POST /api/organizations/current/users (u.description: Pre-existing direct user-creation endpoint now also accepts optional `email` and writes an OrganizationMembership row (previously only set AdminUser fields).

GET /api/organizations/current/export.description: Downloads a JSON bundle of the org's stations, members, event/attendance history, truck-check history (vehicles + check runs with results), and device records (privacy/retention data export). Event history is capped at the most recent 5000 events per station (Q25's 3-month scan cap was fixed 2026-07-17); truck-check runs are capped at the most recent 5000 across all stations combined (Q26, 2026-07-17).

200.description: { exportedAt, organization, stations, members, events, vehicles, truckCheckRuns, devices, limitations } — served as a downloadable attachment

403.description: Caller is not the org owner

org-invites.description: Public org-invite acceptance routes (mounted at /api/org-invites, before any session gate). An invite link is multi-use until expiry/revocation.
org-invites.authentication: None for GET/:token/signup; Bearer JWT for /accept

POST /api/org-invites/:token/accept.description: Signed-in existing user joins the invite's organisation. Bumps usageCount and records invitedBy/inviteId on the new membership.

POST /api/org-invites/:token/signup.description: New account created directly into the invite's organisation with the invite's role. Same response shape as POST /api/auth/signup.

facilities.description: Public emergency-services facility lookup backed by a bundled snapshot of the Digital Atlas of Australia "Emergency Management Facilities" dataset (rural/country fire, metro fire, SES, ambulance, police, other). Used by the signup facility-claim step.

GET /api/facilities/lookup.description: Search/lookup facilities. Requires `q` (min 2 chars) and/or `lat`+`lon`. Each result is annotated with `claimed` (an Organization already holds its facilityKey) — the claiming org's identity is never exposed.

platform.description: Platform-operator administration (not per-org). Gated by the PLATFORM_ADMIN_USERNAMES allowlist (middleware/platformAdmin.ts), not by org role. Covers facility claim-conflict review/resolution, the Q32 organizations console (cross-org visibility — aggregate counts only, never row-level tenant data — plan/status/entitlement management, membership, and destructive delete with a confirm string), and the Q35 station→organization backfill tool. Every mutation writes a PlatformAuditLog row (GET /api/platform/audit-log).

GET /api/platform/organizations.description: List every organization with aggregate, non-PII counts only (stationCount, memberCount, vehicleCount, aiSessionsUsedThisMonth) plus plan/status/billing email. Never returns row-level member/check-in/truck-check/event data.

GET /api/platform/organizations/:id.description: Organization detail (entitlements, facility, aggregate counts) plus its memberships (account username/email/role/lastLoginAt — account info, not tenant content).

200.description: { organization: PlatformOrganizationDetail, members: PlatformOrgMember[], plans: PlanDefinition[] }

PATCH /api/platform/organizations/:id.description: Update an organization's plan, entitlement module toggles, status, or clear its facility claim. Unlike the owner-facing PUT /api/organizations/current, a platform admin may set status directly (e.g. trialing/active/past_due/canceled). Writes an audit log row per changed field group.

DELETE /api/platform/organizations/:id.description: Default: soft-deactivate (status -> canceled). Pass ?hard=true and body {confirm: '<org slug>'} to permanently delete the organization record (does not cascade to stations/members/events).

confirm.description: Must equal the org's slug; required only when ?hard=true

200.description: { organization } for soft delete, { success: true } for hard delete

400.description: Hard delete requested without a matching confirm string

POST /api/platform/organizations/:id/mem.description: Create a new account and membership it into this organization with the given role.

PUT /api/platform/organizations/:id/memb.description: Change a member's role within this organization. Refuses a change that would leave the org with no active owner.

404.description: Member not found in this organisation

409.description: Would leave the organisation with no owner

DELETE /api/platform/organizations/:id/m.description: Remove a member's active membership from this organization. Refuses removal of the last active owner.

404.description: Member not found in this organisation

409.description: Would leave the organisation with no owner

DELETE /api/platform/accounts/:userId.description: Default: soft-deactivate the account. Pass ?hard=true and body {confirm: '<username>'} to permanently delete it (removed from every org's membership list first).

confirm.description: Must equal the account's username; required only when ?hard=true

400.description: Hard delete requested without a matching confirm string

GET /api/platform/stations/orphaned.description: Q35 — list active stations with no organizationId (created before Q33 tagged it on creation), whose members/vehicles don't count toward any org's plan limits until backfilled. Returns name/brigade/hierarchy plus member/vehicle counts so the operator can identify the real-world brigade.

PATCH /api/platform/stations/:id/organiz.description: Q35 — assign (or reassign) a station's owning organization. System stations (demo/default) are rejected. Writes a station.organization_assigned audit log row.

400.description: Missing organizationId, or target is a system station

404.description: Station or target organization not found

GET /api/platform/audit-log.description: List platform-admin audit log entries, newest first (?limit=&offset=, max limit 500). The accountability trail for every mutation made through /api/platform — the hard privacy wall means it is the only record of this access level.

GET /api/platform/claim-conflicts.description: List facility claim conflicts (optionally filtered by ?status=open|resolved), joined with the current holder's org identity.

POST /api/platform/claim-conflicts/:id/r.description: Resolve a conflict: dismiss, mark contacted, or reassign the facility link from the current holder (which becomes a custom/unlinked org) to a target organisation.

404.description: Conflict or reassignment target not found

GET /api/activities.authentication: required (JWT, brigade/kiosk token, member-session, or public demo station) — MASTER_PLAN AC-4, review F1 pattern

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)
Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/activities/active.description: Get currently active activity for station
GET /api/activities/active.authentication: required (JWT, brigade/kiosk token, member-session, or public demo station) — MASTER_PLAN AC-4, review F1 pattern

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)
Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

DELETE /api/activities/:activityId.description: Soft delete an activity (hidden from lists, retained for history). Station-scoped: only activities visible to the requesting station can be deleted.

404.description: Activity not found (or belongs to another station)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/checkins.description: Get all check-ins (both active and inactive)
GET /api/checkins.authentication: required (JWT, brigade/kiosk token, member-session, or public demo station) — MASTER_PLAN AC-4, review F1 pattern

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/checkins/active.authentication: required (JWT, brigade/kiosk token, member-session, or public demo station) — MASTER_PLAN AC-4, review F1 pattern

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/checkins.description: Check in member or undo existing check-in (toggle)

activityId.description: Activity UUID (uses active if not provided)

400.description: Invalid request or no active activity

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)
Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/checkins/url-checkin.description: Check in via sign-in link/QR using member ID (preferred) with legacy name fallback; station-specific

identifier.description: Member ID from sign-in link/QR (preferred); legacy member name accepted for backward compatibility

stationId.description: Station ID for brigade-specific check-in; overrides X-Station-Id header when provided

sessionToken.description: AC-1 — short-lived (8h), station-scoped member-session JWT. Send it back as the X-Member-Session header to read the station's live sign-in book without a full account or kiosk token (station-wide read scope, never more).

AC-1 — short-lived (8h), station-scoped member-session JWT. Send it back as the X-Member-Session header to read the station's live sign-in book without a full account or kiosk token (station-wide read scope, never more).

400.description: Invalid identifier or no active activity

404.description: Member not found for resolved station

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/events.authentication: required (JWT, brigade/kiosk token, member-session, or public demo station) — MASTER_PLAN AC-4, review F1 pattern

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/events/active.authentication: required (JWT, brigade/kiosk token, member-session, or public demo station) — MASTER_PLAN AC-4, review F1 pattern

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/events/:id.authentication: required (JWT, brigade/kiosk token, member-session, or public demo station) — MASTER_PLAN AC-4, review F1 pattern

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)
Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

PUT /api/events/:id/end.description: End an active event (marks as inactive, event remains visible)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

PUT /api/events/:id/reactivate.description: Reactivate an ended event (clears end time, sets isActive=true)
PUT /api/events/:id/reactivate.notes: Events are automatically deactivated after EVENT_EXPIRY_HOURS (default: 12). Use this endpoint to reactivate expired events.

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/events/admin/rollover.description: Manually trigger event expiry check (marks expired events as inactive)
POST /api/events/admin/rollover.notes: Events older than EVENT_EXPIRY_HOURS (default: 12) are automatically marked as inactive. Expired events remain visible in the UI.

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/events/:id/participants.description: Add participant to event (check-in). If participant already exists, toggles and removes them (undo check-in). Creates audit log entry for traceability.

performedBy.description: User ID or name of person performing the action (optional)

notes.description: Optional notes or reason for action (optional)

action.description: Action performed (added or removed if toggled)

400.description: Invalid request or event not active

auditLog.description: Creates EventAuditLog entry for both additions and removals (toggle undo)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/events/:eventId/visitors.description: AC-2 — sign in an ephemeral visitor by typed name. The visitor is recorded as an EventParticipant for attendance but is never persisted as a Member: no history, no member-cap impact, no tap-to-repeat tile. Unlike /participants there is no toggle — every call adds a new visitor row.

name.description: Visitor's typed name (not a Member id)

performedBy.description: User ID or name of person performing the action (optional)

notes.description: Optional notes or reason for action (optional)

400.description: Invalid request, missing name, or event not active

auditLog.description: Creates EventAuditLog entry with action 'participant-added', same as a member check-in

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

DELETE /api/events/:id/participants/:par.description: Remove participant from event (check out). Creates audit log entry for traceability.

performedBy.description: User ID or name of person performing the removal (optional)

notes.description: Optional reason or notes for the removal (optional)

auditLog.description: Creates EventAuditLog entry for compliance and traceability

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/events/:eventId/audit.description: Get audit log of all participant additions and removals for an event. Returns chronological sequence of membership changes.
GET /api/events/:eventId/audit.authentication: required (JWT, brigade/kiosk token, member-session, or public demo station) — MASTER_PLAN AC-4, review F1 pattern

totalLogs.description: Total number of audit log entries

logs.description: Array of audit log entries in chronological order

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/achievements/member/:memberId.description: Get achievements for specific member. Requires a session (requireSession — admin JWT/brigade-token/member-session, or the public demo station); previously ungated (Q36, fixed 2026-07-17).
GET /api/achievements/member/:memberId.authentication: requireSession (JWT any role / brigade-token / member-session / demo)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/achievements/recent.description: Get recently unlocked achievements (all members). Requires a session (requireSession); previously ungated (Q36, fixed 2026-07-17).
GET /api/achievements/recent.authentication: requireSession (JWT any role / brigade-token / member-session / demo)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/reports/attendance-summary.description: Get monthly attendance statistics grouped by month

startDate.description: Start date for report range (ISO 8601). Defaults to 30 days ago.

endDate.description: End date for report range (ISO 8601). Defaults to now.

GET /api/reports/member-participation.description: Get top members by participation count

limit.description: Maximum number of members to return

GET /api/reports/activity-breakdown.description: Get breakdown of activities by category

GET /api/reports/truckcheck-comparative.description: Compare truck check outcomes for the same vehicle type across stations, grouped by the checklist's stable itemCode (TC-D1) so results line up even when brigades word an item differently. When an authenticated org context is present, requested stationIds are scoped down to stations belonging to the caller's organization.

GET /api/stations/lookup.description: Search and locate fire service stations nationally with geolocation sorting. Supports all Australian states and territories.

400.description: Invalid parameters (missing both query and location)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

notes: Returns merged results: closest stations by distance + matching search results
Results are deduplicated by station ID
Distance calculated using Haversine formula
Search is case-insensitive and matches name, suburb, or brigade
Performance target: < 200ms response time

GET /api/stations/count.description: Get total count of loaded fire service stations (debugging/monitoring)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/stations/demo.description: Get the demo station for public viewing (unauthenticated access). Returns only the demo station with sample data for demonstration purposes.

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

notes: Always accessible without authentication
Returns demo-station ID with sample/fake data
Used for public landing page when REQUIRE_AUTH=true

GET /api/stations.description: Get stations with optional filtering and pagination. Org-scoped (Q45, 2026-07-17): when the caller carries an org context, results are limited to that org's own stations plus any not-yet-organizationId-backfilled orphans; fully unscoped (all stations) when there is no org context (kiosk/demo back-compat).

notes: Requires authentication when REQUIRE_AUTH=true environment variable is set

GET /api/stations/brigade/:brigadeId.description: Get all stations in a brigade

200.description: Array of stations in the brigade

notes: Requires authentication when REQUIRE_AUTH=true environment variable is set

GET /api/stations/check-brigade/:brigade.description: Check if a station with this brigade ID already exists. Used for duplicate prevention during station creation.

200.description: Station exists with this brigade ID

message.example: Station "Horsley Park Rural Fire Brigade" already exists with this brigade ID

404.description: No active station found with this brigade ID

message.example: No active station found with this brigade ID

notes: Requires authentication when REQUIRE_AUTH=true environment variable is set

POST /api/stations.description: Create a new station. Validates that brigade ID is unique to prevent duplicate station creation. Returns 409 if a station with the same brigade ID already exists. Tags the new station with the caller's organizationId (from their JWT), if any (Q33, 2026-07-17) — used to scope enforceStationLimit/enforceMemberLimit/enforceVehicleLimit per-org instead of across the whole deployment.

409.description: Station already exists with this brigade ID

message.example: A station with brigade ID "horsley-park-rural-fire-brigade" already exists: Horsley Park Rural Fire Brigade

DELETE /api/stations/:id.description: Soft delete a station (sets isActive=false). Prevents deletion if station has members, active check-ins, or events.

400.description: Cannot delete station with existing data

POST /api/stations/demo/reset.description: Reset demo station with fresh sample data. Deletes all demo station data and recreates it with realistic samples including members, activities, events, and appliances.
notes: Demo station ID is 'demo-station'
Creates 20 diverse sample members with various ranks
Creates 5 standard activities
Creates 10 recent check-ins
Creates 3-4 events (1 active, rest completed)
Creates 2 appliances with sample check runs
Operation may take several seconds to complete
Emits 'demo-station-reset' WebSocket event on success

POST /api/brigade-access/generate.description: Generate a new brigade access token for kiosk mode. Tokens lock the sign-in interface to a specific station.

brigadeId.description: Brigade ID to associate with the token

stationId.description: Station ID to lock access to

description.description: Optional description for the token (e.g., 'Main Kiosk')

expiresInDays.description: Optional expiration period in days (default: no expiration)

kioskUrl.description: Full kiosk sign-in URL with token

notes: No authentication required per current design
Should be protected with admin authentication in production
Tokens are UUIDs (128-bit random) for security

POST /api/brigade-access/validate.description: Validate a brigade access token and return associated station info

name.description: AC-5 device account name (e.g. "Main shed kiosk") — undefined for a legacy anonymous BrigadeAccessToken

type.description: AC-5 device type — undefined for a legacy anonymous BrigadeAccessToken

notes: Public endpoint used by kiosk mode middleware
Expired tokens are automatically removed
Should be protected with admin authentication in production

GET /api/brigade-access/brigade/:brigade.description: Get all active tokens for a brigade

notes: Returns only non-expired tokens
Should be protected with admin authentication in production

GET /api/brigade-access/station/:station.description: Get all active tokens for a station

notes: Returns only non-expired tokens
Should be protected with admin authentication in production
Public endpoint for monitoring
Automatically cleans up expired tokens before counting

GET /api/brigade-access/all-tokens.description: Get all active tokens across all stations (admin utility). Returns tokens with full kiosk URLs for easy distribution.

kioskUrl.description: Full kiosk sign-in URL with embedded token

notes: Master admin utility endpoint
Returns only non-expired tokens
Should be protected with admin authentication in production
Used by /admin/brigade-access frontend page
Automatically cleans up expired tokens before returning results
Each token includes pre-formatted kiosk URL for easy copy/paste
Frontend provides QR code generation and copy-to-clipboard features

GET /api/devices.description: AC-5 — list the caller org's Device accounts (named, typed, revocable kiosk/tablet/phone/wearable credentials), optionally filtered to one station.

stationId.description: Filter to devices enrolled at this station

POST /api/devices.description: AC-5 — enroll a new device for a station in the caller's org. Formalises the anonymous BrigadeAccessToken UUID into a named, typed, revocable credential; the returned token backs the same `/signin?brigade=<token>` kiosk URL.

PATCH /api/devices/:id.description: AC-5 — rename a device and/or flip its status between 'active' and 'revoked'. Only the owning organization may manage its own devices.

403.description: Device belongs to a different organization

DELETE /api/devices/:id.description: AC-5 — remove a device your organization owns.

403.description: Device belongs to a different organization

POST /api/devices/validate.description: AC-5 — public: resolve a device token to its station (called by the device itself, e.g. a kiosk on load). Updates the device's lastSeenAt audit trail on success. Mirrors POST /api/brigade-access/validate, which now falls back to this same resolution (see kioskAccessResolver.ts).

404.description: Token not found, revoked, or expired

notes: Public endpoint — the token itself is the credential, same trust model as /api/brigade-access/validate
Revoked or expired devices resolve as not-found

demo-station-reset.description: Demo station has been reset with fresh data

BillingEvent.description: Audit row for a received Stripe webhook event (including failures), used for idempotency and troubleshooting.

organizationId.description: Org the event applies to; absent when the event carries no org metadata

AARSession.description: A persisted AAR Studio review, scoped to the owning organization. The full client-side session is stored verbatim as a JSON string in payload; the other fields are denormalised for listing and access control. In Table Storage the payload is chunked across payload0..payloadN properties to stay under the per-property/entity size limits; this is an implementation detail not exposed by the API.

id.description: Client-owned review id (AAR Studio generates it)

stationId.description: Optional brigade/station the review belongs to

payload.description: JSON of the full AAR Studio session object

createdBy.description: userId of the facilitator who first saved it

clientUpdatedAt.description: The review's last-edit time on the saving device; used for optimistic-concurrency conflict detection (distinct from updatedAt, the server write time)

ChecklistItem.description: One check on a checklist. itemCode is a stable slug enabling cross-brigade aggregation; isStandard marks an item that comes from a VehicleType's locked standard checklist.

VehicleType.description: A standard vehicle type owning a locked standard checklist. Brigades adopt a type for their vehicles and add/reorder custom items but never edit the standard items (the locked core that enables cross-brigade comparison). isStandard publishes it for other orgs to adopt.

organizationId.description: Owning org; absent for a global/standard type

standardItems.description: Locked standard checks, each with a stable itemCode and isStandard:true

EffectiveChecklist.description: The resolved checklist for an appliance: the vehicle type's locked standard items merged with the brigade's custom overlay, in saved order. Computed, not stored.

items.description: Each carries isStandard so the UI can lock standard rows

isVisitor.description: AC-2: true for ephemeral visitors (typed name, not a persisted Member)

EventAuditLog.description: Audit log entry for event membership changes. Provides complete traceability of participant additions and removals.

id.description: Unique identifier for audit log entry

eventId.description: Event ID this log entry relates to

performedBy.description: User or member who performed the action

memberId.description: Member who was added or removed

memberName.description: Name of member who was added or removed

memberRank.description: Rank of member who was added or removed

deviceInfo.description: Device information captured at time of action

locationInfo.description: Location information captured at time of action

notes.description: Optional notes or reason for action

createdAt.description: When audit log entry was created

name.description: Station name (typically same as brigade name for 1:1 relationships)

distance.description: Distance in kilometers from user location (if location provided)

relevanceScore.description: Search relevance score 0-1 (if search query provided)

BrigadeAccessToken.description: Brigade access token for kiosk mode station locking

stationId.description: Associated station ID - kiosk will be locked to this station

description.description: Optional description for the token (e.g., 'Main Kiosk', 'Mobile Tablet')

expiresAt.description: Optional expiration timestamp - token is invalid after this time

Device.description: AC-5 — a first-class device account (kiosk/tablet/phone/wearable), formalising the anonymous BrigadeAccessToken UUID into a named, typed, revocable credential with a last-seen audit trail.

organizationId.description: Owning org; absent for back-compat (kiosk/demo without org context)

token.description: Unguessable credential — same model as BrigadeAccessToken

lastSeenAt.description: Updated each time the token is validated

runId.description: Linked CheckRun, set once the first result is recorded

organizationId.description: Owning organization from the caller's JWT at session creation; scopes reads to the same tenant (A3 code review F1/F2)

AgentTurn.description: One transcript turn within an AgentSession

toolPayload.description: JSON-serialised tool call/result, present when role is 'tool'

GET /api/auth/entitlements.description: Lightweight entitlement probe for the authenticated user's organisation. Returns the current entitlements object and plan code. Designed for sibling apps (e.g. AAR Studio at /aar) to check access without fetching the full /auth/me payload.

entitlements.description: null when the user has no associated organisation

GET /api/auth/me.description: Current user + organisation + entitlements. Additive fields for multi-org membership and org onboarding: `email` (nullable), `memberships` (all active orgs the user belongs to, each `{organizationId, organizationName, role}`), `isPlatformAdmin`. The existing contract fields (`id`, `username`, `role`, `organizationId`, `organization`, `entitlements`) are unchanged and are relied on by suite siblings (e.g. Fire Break Calculator).

200.description: User + org context, including the new `email`, `memberships`, `isPlatformAdmin` fields

POST /api/auth/signup.description: Self-service sign-up: creates a Community-plan Organization and its owner account. As of the org-onboarding rework, requires `email` and a `facility` selection: either `{facilityKey}` (claims a Digital Atlas of Australia emergency-services facility, first-come-first-served) or `{custom: {name, serviceType, suburb?, state?, postcode?}}` (an unlisted unit — no review needed). Omitting `facility` entirely keeps the legacy behaviour (creates an unlinked/custom org) for back-compat callers.

facility.description: Optional for back-compat; the frontend always sends one variant

201.description: { token, user, organization } — organization includes facilityKey/facilityName/facilityCustom when a facility was resolved

400.description: Missing/invalid fields, unknown facilityKey, or missing custom facility details

409.description: Username already taken, or code=FACILITY_ALREADY_CLAIMED (409) when the chosen facility is already claimed by another organisation — a ClaimConflict row is recorded for the platform admin

503.description: Facility dataset snapshot not loaded (only when a facilityKey is supplied)

PUT /api/auth/profile.description: Update the caller's own profile. Currently just `email` — lets legacy accounts (created before email was required) backfill it.

PUT /api/auth/profile/password.description: Change the caller's own password. Requires the current password to confirm identity — additive to passkey sign-in, never a replacement for it.

400.description: Missing fields, or newPassword shorter than 8 characters

401.description: Not authenticated, or currentPassword is incorrect

POST /api/auth/switch-org.description: Switch the caller's active organisation (multi-org membership). Verifies an active OrganizationMembership in the target org, persists it as the user's default org, and re-issues the JWT with the target organizationId and the membership's per-org role. attachOrganization and all suite consumers keep reading the same claim shape.

200.description: { token, user, organization } scoped to the target org

403.description: Caller is not an active member of that organisation

GET /api/auth/session.description: Phase 2 suite SSO bootstrap. Reads the sk_session httpOnly cookie (set on login/signup, scoped to COOKIE_DOMAIN e.g. .stationkit.com.au) and, if valid, returns the same identity/organization/entitlements payload as GET /api/auth/me plus a freshly-issued bearer token. A sibling app calls this once on load with credentials: 'include' to silently resume an existing Station Manager sign-in, then authorises every subsequent call with the returned token — the cookie itself never authorises a mutation.

entitlements.description: Effective entitlements — plan entitlements with an active standalone Santa Run add-on OR'd in

token.description: Freshly-issued bearer JWT for subsequent Authorization: Bearer calls

401.description: No sk_session cookie, or it is invalid/expired (cleared server-side in the latter case)

POST /api/auth/passkey/register/options.description: Start a passkey registration ceremony for the authenticated user ("Add a passkey" in account settings). Returns WebAuthn PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions (RP id/name = WEBAUTHN_RP_ID/WEBAUTHN_RP_NAME, excludeCredentials = the user's existing passkeys) and stores the challenge server-side, keyed by userId, for a subsequent /register/verify call.

POST /api/auth/passkey/register/verify.description: Verify the browser's attestation response against the challenge from /register/options and, on success, store the new WebAuthnCredential (public key, counter, device type) against the authenticated user. Request body: { response: RegistrationResponseJSON, name?: string }.

400.description: Registration session expired, missing response, or attestation could not be verified

POST /api/auth/passkey/login/options.description: Public. Start a passwordless ("usernameless"/discoverable-credential) sign-in ceremony — no allowCredentials narrowing, so the browser's own picker shows every passkey it holds for WEBAUTHN_RP_ID. Callable cross-origin from any suite app's own login screen (RP id is the shared parent domain), not just this one. Returns an opaque flowId plus WebAuthn PublicKeyCredentialRequestOptions; the challenge is stored server-side keyed by flowId.

POST /api/auth/passkey/login/verify.description: Public. Verify the browser's assertion response against the challenge from /login/options, identify the credential's owning user, bump its signature counter (clone detection), and complete sign-in exactly like POST /api/auth/login — sets the sk_session SSO cookie and returns the same { token, user } shape. Request body: { flowId: string, response: AuthenticationResponseJSON }. The challenge is single-use — replaying the same flowId a second time 400s.

400.description: Missing flowId/response, or the sign-in session has expired/already been used

401.description: Credential id not recognised, assertion could not be verified, or the account is inactive

GET /api/auth/passkey/credentials.description: List the authenticated user's registered passkeys for account-settings display. Never returns the public key.

DELETE /api/auth/passkey/credentials/:id.description: Remove one of the authenticated user's own passkeys. Scoped to the caller — 404s (not 403) for a credential id that exists but belongs to another user, so as not to confirm which ids exist.

404.description: Not found, or owned by a different user

updateTriggers: When adding new REST API endpoints
When modifying endpoint paths, methods, or parameters
When adding or changing Socket.io events

voiceAgentWebSocket.description: Raw (non-Socket.io) WebSocket for the A3 voice-agent push-to-talk flow. Server: attachAgentCheckWs(httpServer) in backend/src/routes/agentCheck.ts.

query.token: JWT, same as Authorization: Bearer (WebSocket upgrade requests can't set headers from the browser)
query.resumeSessionId: Optional. Resumes a still-active session on the same appliance/organization after a dropped connection (A3 code review F9) instead of starting a fresh one.

upgradeChecks: Origin header must be in the configured allowed-origins list when present (A3 code review F6)
Per-user concurrent connection cap, default 3 (429, A3 code review F4)
Appliance's station organization must match the caller's JWT organization (403, A3 code review F1)
aiEnabled entitlement required when entitlements are enabled and the caller has an organization (403, A3 code review F3)

clientToServer.audio-chunk: Base64 PCM16 audio chunk, appended to the buffered utterance
clientToServer.audio-end: Finish the utterance: runs STT, then one agent turn, then TTS
clientToServer.user-text: Typed message instead of voice; runs one agent turn directly
clientToServer.end-session: Client is done; server closes the socket after acknowledging

serverToClient.session-started: { sessionId, resumed: boolean } — resumed is true when resumeSessionId was honoured (A3 code review F9)
serverToClient.transcript: STT result text for the just-finished utterance
serverToClient.agent-text: Agent's spoken reply text, plus whether the run is complete and its runId
serverToClient.agent-audio: TTS audio (MP3) for the agent's reply
serverToClient.busy: Sent instead of a reply when a turn is already in flight for this session (A3 code review F11); client should retry shortly
serverToClient.error: { message } — recoverable errors (provider failure, cap exceeded, bad frame)

voiceAgentWebSocket.liveness: Server pings on an interval and terminates the connection if no pong is received before the next ping (A3 code review F9, dead-connection detection)
